title: RSA Encryption, Signatures, and Safe Migration · Execution Trace: Crypto Go Rsa Padding Oracles
summary: A service encrypts large payloads directly with textbook RSA, verifies PKCS#1 v1.5 signatures by default, and accepts any PEM key without purpose or size validation. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the rsa padding oracles increment in RSA-OAEP and RSA-PSS boundary adapter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Recognize textbook RSA and PKCS#1 v1.5 encryption oracle risks and isolate unavoidable legacy interoperability.
Hash exact canonical bytes and use RSA-PSS with explicit hash and salt policy for new RSA signatures.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Rsa Padding Oracles · Theory Crypto Go Rsa Padding Oracles
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 2724 during a replicated device restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate RSA on trusted keys, OAEP or PSS purpose, payload limits, and uniform failure; keep browser execution deterministic and move production entropy, native timing, non-allowlisted crypto, network, HSM, and compliance claims to authorized transfer gates; then double attacker-controlled size or work and remain inside the admission budget. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Crypto Go Rsa Padding Oracles contract for the supplied data.
